Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

As a creative person who sometimes gets overwhelmed by intense debates, I find that staying calm is key even if my neuroticism score isn't the lowest thing in the world. For instance, when someone starts talking about COVID vaccines at work or during family gatherings back home near Milan, where we're all used to getting involved in things together anyway, but now with this health crisis everyone has strong opinions.\n\nMy dad actually has a PhD and he's super smart, so sometimes I ask him for quick pointers on science stuff without realizing that might come off as taking his opinion when they don't represent mine. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
I think it's really important to approach these kinds of conversations carefully because emotions run high on topics like this. As a creative person who sometimes gets overwhelmed by intense debates, I find that staying calm is key even if my neuroticism score isn't the lowest thing in the world. For instance, when someone starts talking about COVID vaccines at work or during family gatherings back home near Milan, where we're all used to getting involved in things together anyway, but now with this health crisis everyone has strong opinions.

My dad actually has a PhD and he's super smart, so sometimes I ask him for quick pointers on science stuff without realizing that might come off as taking his opinion when they don't represent mine. And since my agreeableness level is moderate according to Big Five traits—meaning I try not to rock boats too much—I usually go along with what people say unless it really doesn't make sense from a health perspective.

But let's be honest, dealing with vaccine discussions can feel like navigating through a minefield sometimes. On the plus side though? I love board games and running; those help clear my head after tough debates online or in person.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Based on being creative (psychological feature), native-born Italian background with family ties requiring diplomacy (agreeableness moderate level, conscientiousness low score), daily social media use which exposes one to diverse opinions but also stress given high neuroticism. Used examples from work and family life in Milan.
